# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains controlled benchmark evidence about LLM agent memory systems, comparing write strategies and retrieval methods, with measured accuracy, retrieval precision, utilization behavior, failure modes, computational cost notes, and stated limitations.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Yuan et al., Abstract; §1; §2.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper introduces a diagnostic framework that separately measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes in memory-augmented LLM agents.

## Source Excerpt

"We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes how performance differences manifest across write strategies, retrieval methods, and memory utilization behavior" and "propose a diagnostic probing framework that sits at the retrieval-to-generation boundary and independently measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes."

## Note

This is a framework description, not a performance result.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Yuan et al., Abstract; §1; Appendix B
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The evaluation uses a 3 × 3 factorial design crossing three write strategies with three retrieval methods.

## Source Excerpt

"We conduct a controlled 3 × 3 factorial study that crosses three write strategies (raw chunks, Mem0-style extraction, MemGPT-style summarization) with three retrieval methods (cosine similarity, BM25, hybrid reranking)." Appendix B also states, "We adopt a 3 × 3 factorial design that crosses three write strategies with three retrieval methods."

## Note

The source describes this as a controlled comparison design.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Yuan et al., §1; Appendix B
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study evaluates 1,540 non-adversarial questions from LoCoMo.

## Source Excerpt

"Evaluating on LoCoMo (1,540 non-adversarial questions)" and Appendix B states, "We evaluate on 1,540 non-adversarial questions."

## Note

The source also describes LoCoMo as 10 multi-session conversations with roughly 200 questions per conversation.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., Abstract; Table 1; §3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Average accuracy varies from 57.1% to 77.2% across retrieval methods.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states, "average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%)." Table 1 reports average accuracy of 73.4 for cosine, 57.1 for BM25, and 77.2 for hybrid.

## Note

The reported span is across retrieval methods, not across write strategies.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., Abstract; §1; §3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Within a retrieval method, write strategy changes accuracy by only 3 to 8 percentage points.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states, "average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%) but only 3–8 points across write strategies." Section 3.1 says, "write strategy accounts for only 3–8 accuracy points within any retrieval column."

## Note

This evidence compares within-column write-strategy spread, not end-to-end cost.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: Yuan et al., §2.1; Appendix B Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Basic RAG stores raw 3-turn conversation chunks with speaker names and timestamps and requires zero LLM calls at write time.

## Source Excerpt

"Basic RAG stores raw 3-turn conversation chunks with speaker names and timestamps, requiring zero LLM calls at write time." Appendix B Table 3 lists "Write Basic RAG Store raw 3-turn chunks None."

## Note

This evidence describes the write strategy configuration used in the experiment.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., Table 1; §3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Basic RAG achieved 77.9% accuracy with cosine retrieval and 81.1% with hybrid retrieval.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 reports "Basic RAG ... 77.9" under cosine accuracy and "81.1" under hybrid accuracy. Section 3.1 states, "Basic RAG ... achieves 77.9% under cosine and 81.1% under hybrid."

## Note

These are the reported accuracy values for the raw-chunk write strategy under two retrieval methods.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., §3.1; Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Under BM25, Summarized Episodes scored 62.7% accuracy versus 59.2% for Basic RAG.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.1 states, "The one exception is BM25, where Summarized Episodes (62.7%) edges out Basic RAG (59.2%), likely because keyword retrieval benefits from the denser vocabulary of compressed summaries." Table 1 reports BM25 accuracy of 62.7 for Summ. Episodes and 59.2 for Basic RAG.

## Note

This is an exception to the broader pattern that raw chunk storage matches or exceeds the more expensive write strategies. The source gives only a likely explanation, not a directly established mechanism.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., §3.2; Figure 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Retrieval precision and downstream accuracy are reported as near-perfectly correlated, with r = 0.98.

## Source Excerpt

"Retrieval precision is near-perfectly correlated with downstream accuracy (r = 0.98, Figure 4), providing strong evidence that retrieval quality most strongly correlates with downstream performance." The figure caption states, "Precision@5 vs. accuracy (r = 0.98)."

## Note

The source reports correlation, not causal proof.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., §3.3; Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Retrieval failure accounted for 11% to 46% of questions across configurations.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3 states, "Retrieval failure is the dominant error mode, accounting for 11–46% of all questions depending on configuration." Table 2 shows retrieval failure rates ranging from 11.4 to 46.3 across the nine settings.

## Note

The upper end corresponds to BM25 with Extracted Facts; the lower end corresponds to Basic RAG with Hybrid.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., §3.3; Table 2; Figure 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
For Basic RAG, hybrid reranking reduced retrieval failure from 35.3% under BM25 to 11.4%.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3 states, "Hybrid reranking cuts retrieval failures roughly in half (e.g., 35.3% to 11.4% for Basic RAG)." Table 2 reports "Basic RAG BM25 ... Retrieval 35.3" and "Basic RAG Hybrid ... Retrieval 11.4."

## Note

This evidence is a configuration-specific failure-mode comparison.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., §3.3; Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Across configurations, utilization failures remained between 4% and 8%, and hallucinations remained between 0.4% and 1.4%.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3 states, "By contrast, utilization failures remain stable at 4–8% and hallucinations at 0.4–1.4% regardless of configuration." Table 2 lists utilization values from 3.9 to 8.1 and hallucination values from 0.4 to 1.4.

## Note

The text rounds utilization failures to 4–8%, while the table includes one 3.9% value.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: claim-support
citation: Yuan et al., Limitations; Appendix D
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The study states that its conclusions are limited by use of a single backbone model, one benchmark, fixed retrieval budget k = 5, prompt-based write strategies, and LLM judges for correctness and failure classification.

## Source Excerpt

The limitations section states, "Our study evaluates a single backbone model (GPT-5-mini) on one benchmark (LoCoMo) with a fixed retrieval budget (k = 5), limiting generality. The write strategies are prompt-based reimplementations rather than fully learned memory systems... Additionally, answer correctness and failure classification rely on LLM judges." Appendix D reports the judge validation results.

## Note

This evidence attaches scope limits and evaluation caveats to the reported findings.
---

evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Yuan et al., §3.1; Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Under BM25, Summarized Episodes scored 62.7% accuracy versus 59.2% for Basic RAG.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.1 states, "The one exception is BM25, where Summarized Episodes (62.7%) edges out Basic RAG (59.2%), likely because keyword retrieval benefits from the denser vocabulary of compressed summaries." Table 1 reports BM25 accuracy of 62.7 for Summ. Episodes and 59.2 for Basic RAG.

## Note

This is an exception to the broader pattern that raw chunk storage matches or exceeds the more expensive write strategies. The source gives only a likely explanation, not a directly established mechanism.
```

---

Retrieval failure accounted for 11% to 46% of questions across configurations.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3 states, "Retrieval failure is the dominant error mode, accounting for 11–46% of all questions depending on configuration." Table 2 shows retrieval failure rates ranging from 11.4 to 46.3 across the nine settings.

The upper end corresponds to BM25 with Extracted Facts; the lower end corresponds to Basic RAG with Hybrid.
```

### file: evidence-11.md

contradiction_relevance: supports
---
For Basic RAG, hybrid reranking reduced retrieval failure from 35.3% under BM25 to 11.4%.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3 states, "Hybrid reranking cuts retrieval failures roughly in half (e.g., 35.3% to 11.4% for Basic RAG)." Table 2 reports "Basic RAG BM25 ... Retrieval 35.3" and "Basic RAG Hybrid ... Retrieval 11.4."

## Note

This evidence is a configuration-specific failure-mode comparison.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---

contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Across configurations, utilization failures remained between 4% and 8%, and hallucinations remained between 0.4% and 1.4%.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3 states, "By contrast, utilization failures remain stable at 4–8% and hallucinations at 0.4–1.4% regardless of configuration." Table 2 lists utilization values from 3.9 to 8.1 and hallucination values from 0.4 to 1.4.

## Note

The text rounds utilization failures to 4–8%, while the table includes one 3.9% value.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: claim-support
citation: Yuan et al., Limitations; Appendix D
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The study states that its conclusions are limited by use of a single backbone model, one benchmark, fixed retrieval budget k = 5, prompt-based write strategies, and LLM judges for correctness and failure classification.

## Source Excerpt

The limitations section states, "Our study evaluates a single backbone model (GPT-5-mini) on one benchmark (LoCoMo) with a fixed retrieval budget (k = 5), limiting generality. The write strategies are prompt-based reimplementations rather than fully learned memory systems... Additionally, answer correctness and failure classification rely on LLM judges." Appendix D reports the judge validation results.

## Note

This evidence attaches scope limits and evaluation caveats to the reported findings.

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains controlled benchmark evidence about LLM agent memory systems, comparing write strategies and retrieval methods, with measured accuracy, retrieval precision, utilization behavior, failure modes, computational cost notes, and stated limitations.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-08-002
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Yuan et al., Abstract; §1; §2.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02473
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper introduces a diagnostic framework that separately measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes in memory-augmented LLM agents.

## Source Excerpt

"We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes how performance differences manifest across write strategies, retrieval methods, and memory utilization behavior" and "propose a diagnostic probing framework that sits at the retrieval-to-generation boundary and independently measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes."

## Note

This is a framework description, not a performance result.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1

mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The evaluation uses a 3 × 3 factorial design crossing three write strategies with three retrieval methods.

## Source Excerpt

"We conduct a controlled 3 × 3 factorial study that crosses three write strategies (raw chunks, Mem0-style extraction, MemGPT-style summarization) with three retrieval methods (cosine similarity, BM25, hybrid reranking)." Appendix B also states, "We adopt a 3 × 3 factorial design that crosses three write strategies with three retrieval methods."

## Note

The source describes this as a controlled comparison design.
```

Average accuracy varies from 57.1% to 77.2% across retrieval methods.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states, "average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%)." Table 1 reports average accuracy of 73.4 for cosine, 57.1 for BM25, and 77.2 for hybrid.

## Note

The reported span is across retrieval methods, not across write strategies.
```

Within a retrieval method, write strategy changes accuracy by only 3 to 8 percentage points.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states, "average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%) but only 3–8 points across write strategies." Section 3.1 says, "write strategy accounts for only 3–8 accuracy points within any retrieval column."

## Note

This evidence compares within-column write-strategy spread, not end-to-end cost.
```
